criteria.criterion: The generated UI has a clear visual thesis, one primary action, purposeful motion, and desktop/mobile verification notes.

customizationQuestion: What should Spanks help you build with the OpenAI frontend guide?
extractedSummary: OpenAI's guide turns GPT-5.4 frontend work into a deliberate workflow: start with constraints, define a visual thesis, generate a strong prompt, build with Codex, then verify with browser screenshots and litmus checks.

gotchas.description: If the prompt only asks for a generic page, Codex may produce safe card-heavy UI. Add visual constraints, a clear audience, and a specific mood.
gotchas.fix: Include typography, color, composition, interaction, and verification constraints before asking Codex to build.
gotchas.title: Weak prompts create generic UI

promptSnippets: Design a frontend with a strong visual thesis, restrained composition, and explicit verification.
Use browser screenshots to check desktop and mobile output before calling it done.
Avoid generic cards, weak branding, and motion that does not communicate state or story.

skill.name: openai-delightful-frontends
skill.title: Build a delightful frontend with GPT-5.4
skill.yamlContent:
name: openai-delightful-frontends
steps:
  - title: Identify the frontend job
  - title: Set design constraints
  - title: Generate the Codex prompt
  - title: Run Codex in a demo workspace
  - title: Verify in browser

steps.description: Read the source and identify the exact frontend job, audience, and intended emotional response.
steps.gotchaText: Do not start coding before the page has a single clear job.
steps.title: Identify the frontend job
steps.verification: The prompt names the product, audience, and primary action.

steps.description: Choose a visual thesis: typography, color world, layout rhythm, motion role, and one thing the page must not do.
steps.gotchaText: A vague mood like modern or sleek is not enough for a distinctive result.
steps.title: Set design constraints
steps.verification: The constraints specify brand, typography, composition, color, and motion.

steps.description: Generate a Codex-ready prompt that tells Codex what to build, how it should feel, what to avoid, and how to verify it.
steps.gotchaText: Include verification in the prompt so Codex does not stop after code edits.
steps.title: Generate the Codex prompt
steps.verification: The generated prompt includes build instructions and browser verification criteria.

steps.description: Run Codex in the dedicated Pawscript demo workspace, not inside the Pawscript app repo.
steps.gotchaText: Never let the demo mutate the running Pawscript app unless the user explicitly chooses that folder.
steps.title: Run Codex safely

steps.description: Open the result and check desktop and mobile quality against the guide: clear hierarchy, one primary action, strong brand, and no generic filler cards.
steps.gotchaText: If it looks like a default template, rerun with sharper visual constraints.
steps.title: Verify the result
steps.verification: Desktop and mobile screenshots satisfy the guide's litmus checks.
